title: Tapping for Body and Burnout
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the exhaustion that has settled into the body rather than just the mind
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for burnout that lives in the body: the persistent physical exhaustion that does not clear with rest, the dragging weight of a system that has been running too hard for too long. Works through the physical and emotional layers toward a gentler relationship with what the body needs.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the exhaustion is physical as well as mental, and you have been telling yourself to push through it for longer than is reasonable.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for the burnout that has landed in the body. The script works through the physical exhaustion and the push-through habit, toward a place where the body's needs are allowed to count.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though my body is exhausted and I keep ignoring it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I have been pushing through for a long time and the body is paying for it, I am open to stopping and listening.
Even though rest feels like something I have to earn, I accept where I am right now.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The exhaustion that doesn't clear even when I sleep.
Side of Eye: My body has been running on empty.
Under Eye: The weight of carrying everything for too long.
Under Nose: The guilt of stopping feels bigger than the exhaustion.
Chin: My body is asking for something I keep overriding.
Collarbone: The habit of pushing through because there is always more to do.
Under Arm: Carrying the tiredness as if it is a character flaw.
Top of Head: How long this has been going on.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: My body tells me what it needs. I can start listening.
Side of Eye: Rest is not weakness. It is maintenance.
Under Eye: I am allowed to stop before I collapse.
Under Nose: The work will still be there. The body will not always wait.
Chin: My body is on my side. It is trying to help.
Collarbone: I can choose one thing today that gives the body what it is asking for.
Under Arm: I give myself permission to be less than fully functional right now.
Top of Head: I am coming back to myself. Slowly is fine.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
